published.label: Published now
published.definition: Usable public information that currently meets TriWei's source, attribution, and authorship requirements.

workbench.label: Research workbench
workbench.definition: A useful feature concept that is not published in its intended form until its evidence, authorship, and review gate is satisfied.

retired.label: Retired method
retired.definition: An approach TriWei has decided not to use because it conflicts with the publication standard or creates a misleading impression.

published.title: Research Lineage Library
published.available_now: Direct arXiv abstract links, exact titles, named authors, identifiers, dates, and provisional chronological reading paths.
published.current_limit: No AI paper summaries or unreviewed claims that one paper influenced, proved, or superseded another.

published.title: AI Hardware Source Register
published.available_now: Source-dated manufacturer specifications and direct materials, manufacturing, and fab-project references.
published.current_limit: No generated purchasing advice, universal rankings, or claims that unlike architectures are directly comparable.

published.title: Industry Data Register
published.available_now: First-party API prices, source-dated supply-chain references, and attributed model-access records.
published.current_limit: No automated market narrative, significance ranking, or frequency-based company score.

published.title: Ethics and Governance Sources
published.available_now: Direct links to public-institution records concerning governance, risk, copyright, and accountability.
published.current_limit: No automated ethical rating, legal conclusion, harm finding, or article synopsis.

workbench.title: Evidence-based Signal History
workbench.intended_value: A traceable visual chronology showing explicit relationships among papers, releases, tests, criticism, and later applications.
workbench.blocked_by: The former relationship labels and historical narrative were AI-assisted and have not been independently researched and human-authored.
acceptance_gate: A human reviews every source in the proposed chronology.
Every relationship is supported by an explicit citation or statement in the linked works.
The chronology names its human author and review date.
Competing accounts and unresolved priority questions remain visible.

workbench.title: Visual Hardware Explainers
workbench.intended_value: Diagrams that help readers understand memory, interconnect, power, packaging, fabrication, and system-level tradeoffs.
workbench.blocked_by: The removed explanatory lessons were AI-assisted and could imply comparability or practical conclusions not established by the cited specifications.
acceptance_gate: A human authors each explanation from primary technical documentation.
Vendor-specific terminology and non-comparable measurements are identified.
Every diagram has source and reuse provenance.
The page avoids purchasing, safety, or deployment advice unless separately qualified and reviewed.

workbench.title: Industry and Supply-chain Visualizations
workbench.intended_value: Source-dated maps of physical production stages, model access, pricing, and documented industry relationships.
workbench.blocked_by: The removed visual readouts mixed source data with generated interpretation, media-frequency signals, and unsupported market conclusions.
acceptance_gate: Every displayed relationship has an inspectable primary source.
Measurements retain units, reference periods, and known limitations.
No media-frequency or source-count field is presented as importance, market power, or truth.
Human-authored interpretation is visibly separated from source data.

workbench.title: Human-authored Ethics Watch
workbench.intended_value: Careful, attributed analysis of governance, rights, labor, safety, copyright, and accountability developments.
workbench.blocked_by: The former heat map and tags could be read as automated judgments of harm, legality, safety, intent, or ethical merit.
acceptance_gate: A human identifies the exact question being examined.
Relevant legal, technical, social, and affected-party sources are included.
Claims and allegations remain attributed to their source.
The analysis states jurisdictional, evidentiary, and professional-advice limits.

workbench.title: TriWei Daily Brief
workbench.intended_value: A small set of concrete, source-linked developments selected for readers who need a manageable entry point.
workbench.available_label: Use the rotating original-source folders now
workbench.blocked_by: Automated clustering previously converted recurring names and coverage volume into generic significance claims.
acceptance_gate: Every item is a specific documented event rather than an entity or keyword.
Selection criteria are published and do not equate recurrence with significance.
Any synopsis is independently researched and human-authored.
Primary sources, uncertainty, corrections, and conflicting evidence are visible.

retired.title: AI-written article synopses
retired.reason: TriWei will not substitute generated paraphrases for third-party authors' work when TriWei has not independently researched and human-authored the analysis.

retired.title: Coverage frequency as importance or truth
retired.reason: Repeated collection may reflect feed composition, publicity, duplication, or attention. It does not establish factual accuracy, consequence, or merit.

retired.title: Automated ethical heat scores or verdicts
retired.reason: Keywords and machine tags cannot establish harm, intent, legality, safety, accountability, or ethical merit.

retired.title: Unreviewed historical or scholarly relationship claims
retired.reason: Chronology, similarity, and thematic proximity do not by themselves establish influence, priority, replication, criticism, or causation.
